description_long: This object is a classic gramophone-style record player featuring a large, flared brass horn mounted on a wooden base. The base is rectangular and constructed from dark, polished wood, with a visible crank handle on one side for manual operation. Atop the base sits a circular turntable platter, which appears to be black with a blue label at its center, and a tonearm extending over it. The horn is highly reflective, metallic, and golden in color, curving upward and outward to amplify sound. The overall design is vintage, evoking early 20th-century aesthetics, and the object is detailed with visible joints and mechanical parts.
description: A vintage gramophone record player with a large brass horn, wooden base, and visible turntable platter and tonearm. The design is ornate and classic, with a polished finish and mechanical details.
description_view_0: The front view shows the brass horn prominently angled to the left, the crank handle on the right side, and the turntable platter with the tonearm resting over it.
description_view_1: The rear view highlights the back of the horn and the wooden base, with the crank handle extending out to the left and less detail visible on the platter.
description_view_2: The left side view displays the full curve of the horn, the tonearm extending over the platter, and the layered wooden base.
description_view_3: The top-down view looks directly into the bell of the horn, showing its flared shape and the circular platter below, with the crank handle visible on the right.
attribute_turntable_platter: The turntable platter is present, circular, and black with a blue central label. It is proportionally large compared to the base, occupying most of the top surface. The platter appears smooth with a slight sheen, and no visible grooves or patterns are apparent.
attribute_tonearm: The tonearm is present, extending from the right side of the base over the platter. It is slender, metallic, and appears to be made of steel or brass, with a matte finish. The tonearm is resting over the platter and has a visible joint near its base.
attribute_stylus: The stylus is present, attached to the end of the tonearm. It is small, pointed, and metallic, appearing as a distinct component at the tip of the tonearm.
